perturbation.original_text: THIS AGREEMENT is dated as of the 26th day of September, 2007 AMONG: SWANSI HOLDINGS CORP. , a Panama corporation and having an office for business located at Nerine Fiduciaire S.A., Rue des Terreaux-du-Temple 4, Case postale 5023, CH - 1211 Geneva 11, Switzerland (“Swansi”); AND: ZULU ENERGY CORP. , a Colorado corporation having an office for business located at 2610– 1066 West Hastings Street, Vancouver, BC V6E 3X2 (“Zulu”)
perturbation.changed_text: THIS AGREEMENT is dated as of the 26th day of September, 2007 AMONG: SWANSI HOLDINGS CORP. , a Panama corporation and having an office for business located at Nerine Fiduciaire S.A., Rue des Terreaux-du-Temple 4, Case postale 5023, CH - 1211 Geneva 11, Switzerland (“Swansi” also referred to as the “Seller”); AND: ZULU ENERGY CORP. , a Colorado corporation having an office for business located at 2610– 1066 West Hastings Street, Vancouver, BC V6E 3X2 (“Zulu” also referred to as the “Buyer”)
perturbation.explanation: The terms 'Swansi' and 'Zulu' are explicitly defined. However, adding 'also referred to as the Seller' for 'Swansi' and 'also referred to as the Buyer' for 'Zulu' without consistently using these new terms creates confusion. It makes 'Swansi' and 'Zulu' have another identity. This contradiction is evident as the agreement later refers to specific obligations of 'Swansi' and 'Zulu', and it becomes unclear if those obligations also apply to the 'Seller' and 'Buyer' in all instances.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS Swansi and Zulu are willing to enter into discussions regarding a possible acquisition by Zulu of shares of Nyati Resources Botswana (PTY) Limited, a Botswana corporation (“Nyati Botswana”) from Swansi (the “Acquisition”). NOW THEREFORE THIS AGREEMENT WITNESSETH THAT in consideration of the exchange of Confidential Information (as defined herein) between Swansi and Zulu as may be reasonably requested from time to time and other good and valuable consideration, the receipt and sufficiency of which is hereby acknowledged, the parties hereto hereby agree as follows:
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces the terms 'Seller' and 'Buyer' as alternatives to 'Swansi' and 'Zulu', respectively. The contradicted text only refers to 'Swansi' and 'Zulu'. This creates a Misaligned Terminology contradiction because it becomes unclear if the obligations and rights assigned to 'Swansi' and 'Zulu' in the contradicted text also apply to the 'Seller' and 'Buyer'. This is due to the lack of consistent and explicit interchangeability between the terms throughout the entire document, creating a confusion for enforcability.

perturbation.original_text: Representatives 1.2 For purposes of this Agreement, the term representatives (“Representatives”) shall mean each party, their directors, officers and employees, as well as their counsel, accountants, consultants and other representatives in connection with the transactions contemplated hereby provided that such persons are bound by confidentiality agreements no less stringent than those in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Representatives 1.2 For purposes of this Agreement, the term representatives (“Representatives”) also referred to as (“Agents”) shall mean each party, their directors, officers and employees, as well as their counsel, accountants, consultants and other representatives in connection with the transactions contemplated hereby provided that such persons are bound by confidentiality agreements no less stringent than those in this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Adding the term 'Agents' as also referred as 'Representatives' creates contradiction. The text throughout the contract mentions 'Representatives' but not 'Agents.' This creates an ambiguous obligation on whether 'Agents' are also bound, especially in sections like Non-Disclosure and Use of Confidential Information, since 'Agents' are not explicitely specified or used throughout the text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Use of Confidential Information 1.3 Each party will use the Confidential Information it receives solely for the purpose of evaluating the Acquisition and not for any other purpose and, except to the extent permitted by paragraph 1.5 hereof, will keep such Confidential Information strictly confidential, provided, however, that Confidential Information may be disclosed to such Representatives as needed to know such information for the purpose of evaluating and negotiating the terms of the Acquisition and for no other purpose.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces 'Agents' as also referred to as 'Representatives'. The contradicted text only refers to 'Representatives'. This creates ambiguity as to whether the obligations in the contradicted text extend to 'Agents', creating a strong in-text contradiction because it is unclear whether 'Agents' are bound by the same restrictions as 'Representatives' regarding Confidential Information.

perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information 1.1 For purposes of this Agreement, the term confidential information (“Confidential Information”) shall mean all financial and other nonpublic information, together with notes, analyses, compilations, studies or other documents prepared or provided by the disclosing party in connection with the evaluation of the Acquisition.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information 1.1 For purposes of this Agreement, the term confidential information (“Confidential Information”) also referred to as 'Protected Data' shall mean all financial and other nonpublic information, together with notes, analyses, compilations, studies or other documents prepared or provided by the disclosing party in connection with the evaluation of the Acquisition.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'Protected Data' as another term for 'Confidential Information' creates a contradiction because subsequent sections refer to 'Confidential Information' but not 'Protected Data'. This raises questions about whether obligations related to 'Confidential Information' also implicitly apply to 'Protected Data'. This introduces uncertainty, especially in sections outlining data handling and security measures.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party will use the Confidential Information it receives solely for the purpose of evaluating the Acquisition and not for any other purpose and, except to the extent permitted by paragraph 1.5 hereof, will keep such Confidential Information strictly confidential, provided, however, that Confidential Information may be disclosed to such Representatives as needed to know such information for the purpose of evaluating and negotiating the terms of the Acquisition and for no other purpose.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces 'Protected Data' as an alternative term for 'Confidential Information'. The contradicted text explicitly refers to 'Confidential Information' and outlines specific obligations related to its use and protection. Because the contradicted section does not mention 'Protected Data', it creates uncertainty regarding whether the obligations pertaining to 'Confidential Information' also apply to 'Protected Data'. This misalignment of terminology creates a strong contradiction and ambiguity, potentially affecting the enforceability of the contract regarding data handling, which aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: ARTICLE 2 STANDSTILL Zulu and its principal shareholders agree that until October 31, 2007, or such shorter period if either party notifies the other that it no longer wishes to proceed with the Acquisition (“Term of this Agreement”), that they will not, directly or indirectly, solicit, initiate or encourage submission of proposals of offers from any third party relating to any acquisition, purchase or option to purchase an equity interest in Zulu, or any merger, consolidation or business combination with Zulu or the sale of substantially all of the assets of Zulu.
perturbation.changed_text: ARTICLE 2 STANDSTILL Zulu and its principal shareholders agree that until October 31, 2007, or such shorter period if either party notifies the other that it no longer wishes to proceed with the Acquisition (“Term of this Agreement” also know as “Restriction Period”), that they will not, directly or indirectly, solicit, initiate or encourage submission of proposals of offers from any third party relating to any acquisition, purchase or option to purchase an equity interest in Zulu, or any merger, consolidation or business combination with Zulu or the sale of substantially all of the assets of Zulu.
perturbation.explanation: By introducing 'Restriction Period' as a synonymous term for 'Term of this Agreement' within the standstill clause, we create a potential contradiction. Subsequent clauses refer to 'Term of this Agreement', so it is unclear whether the same stipulations apply during the 'Restriction Period', potentially affecting the scope and enforceability of the standstill obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties hereto agree that they shall not solicit business from any supplier, customer, client or contact of any other party hereto for the purpose of circumventing the relationship between such party and such supplier, customer, client and/or competing with such party during the Term of this Agreement without prior written consent.
perturbation.justification: The change introduces "Restriction Period" as synonymous with "Term of this Agreement." The contradicted text refers only to "Term of this Agreement". If 'Restriction Period' and 'Term of this Agreement' have different meanings (even subtly), it creates ambiguity and contradiction as to which period the restriction applies. This is a strong contradiction according to the definition of Misaligned Terminology.
